skill.name: study-design-scale-selector
skill.description: Determines the appropriate Risk of Bias assessment scale for a medical study based on its design (RCT, Cohort, etc.), using PubMed metadata lookup or text analysis. Use when the user wants to know which quality assessment tool to use for a specific paper (given PMID or abstract).
skill.summary: Selects the correct risk of bias assessment tool for medical studies using PubMed lookup or text analysis of study design
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 81 static analysis findings across 5 files. Confirmed 47 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings as FALSE POSITIVES (the scanner misidentified markdown text and non-cryptographic string operations as crypto usage; no actual cryptographic operations exist in the codebase). Confirmed 4 'system reconnaissance' findings as FALSE POSITIVES (standard input sanitization and argument parsing). 30 true positive findings relate to external command execution (SKILL.md bash workflow instructions), 1 filesystem write (PDF text extraction), and 3 network calls (PubMed API) - all legitimate and expected for this medical research skill. No malicious intent, obfuscation, data exfiltration, or credential exposure detected.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs execution of Python scripts via bash commands (e.g., 'python scripts/selector.py <PMID>', 'python scripts/extract_pdf.py'). All 29 flagged locations are legitimate workflow instructions for running the skill's own scripts. Commands reference known packaged scripts only. No arbitrary command injection vector found. Confidence: 0.85 - Pattern is a true positive but the behavior is intentional and scoped to the skill's packaged scripts.

medium_findings.title: Network Access to PubMed API
medium_findings.description: selector.py makes HTTP requests to the public NCBI PubMed API at eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov. This is core functionality for fetching medical study metadata by PMID. URL is hardcoded to a legitimate government research endpoint. Timeout is set to 10 seconds. No data exfiltration or credential transmission. Confidence: 0.90 - Confirmed network call to a well-known, legitimate public research API.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Write for PDF Text Extraction
medium_findings.description: scripts/extract_pdf.py writes extracted PDF text to disk using open(). Output filename is auto-generated from input filename. No arbitrary path injection; the path comes from a validated CLI argument. This is standard PDF text extraction functionality. Confidence: 0.88 - Confirmed file write but for a legitimate data processing purpose.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 47 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' across all files. Inspection confirms these are all false positives triggered by non-cryptographic strings (markdown file paths containing '.md', JSON data, documentation text, and Python string operations like ', '.join()). No actual cryptographic algorithms are used anywhere in this skill. Confidence: 0.98 - Definitively false positive; no crypto imports or operations exist in the codebase.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 4 instances of 'System reconnaissance'. These are standard input processing operations: pmid.strip() for whitespace sanitization and sys.argv[1] for reading command-line arguments. No actual system enumeration, process listing, or reconnaissance behavior exists. Confidence: 0.95 - Standard argument parsing and string sanitization, not reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Select Risk of Bias Scales for Medical Research Studies
content.value_statement: Medical researchers often struggle to select the correct quality assessment tool for different study designs. This skill identifies the study design of a medical paper and returns the appropriate risk of bias assessment scale using PubMed metadata or text analysis.
actual_capabilities: Fetches study metadata from PubMed using a PMID to determine study design
Identifies study design from text analysis when no PMID is available
Maps study designs to the correct risk of bias assessment scale using reference rules
Extracts text from PDF files for full-text screening and analysis
Returns structured JSON output with study design and selected scale name
limitations: Requires a valid PMID or detailed abstract for accurate study design detection
Does not perform the actual risk of bias assessment, only selects the appropriate scale
PubMed API dependency may fail for very recently published or unpublished studies
PDF text extraction quality depends on the PDF format and may miss embedded content

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Scale Selection
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a systematic review needs to select the correct quality assessment tool for each included study based on its design
use_cases.target_user: Medical researcher or systematic review author

use_cases.title: Rapid Study Design Classification
use_cases.description: A student needs to quickly determine the study design and appropriate bias assessment scale for multiple papers during a literature review

use_cases.title: Meta-Analysis Methodology Planning
use_cases.description: A meta-analyst needs to verify the correct risk of bias tool for each study design type before beginning a pooled analysis

prompt_templates.title: Quick Scale Lookup by PMID
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to find the right risk of bias assessment scale for a study. The PMID is 32512345. What study design does this paper use and which scale should I apply?
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher has a PMID and needs immediate scale selection

prompt_templates.title: Text-Based Study Design Identification
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an abstract but no PMID for a study. The study describes following a group of smokers and non-smokers over 10 years to track lung cancer incidence. Which study design is this and what assessment scale should I use?
prompt_templates.scenario: User has only abstract text and needs design classification

prompt_templates.title: PDF Full-Text Scale Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a PDF of a study at /data/papers/study2024.pdf. Extract the text, determine the study design, and tell me which risk of bias scale to use for assessment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher has a full PDF and wants automated extraction and scale selection

prompt_templates.title: Batch Study Design Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: I have three studies with PMIDs 12345678, 23456789, and 34567890. For each one, determine the study design and recommend the appropriate risk of bias assessment scale. Present the results as a comparison table.
prompt_templates.scenario: Systematic reviewer needs comparative analysis for multiple studies

output_examples.input: What scale should I use for PMID 25698745?
output: Recommended Scale: RoB 2 (Cochrane Risk of Bias tool for randomized trials)
The study was classified as an RCT based on PubMed publication type metadata.

output_examples.input: I have a study that compares surgical outcomes between two groups retrospectively. No PMID available.
output: Study Design: Non-randomised study of interventions

Based on the description of retrospective comparison between intervention groups, the study is classified as a non-randomised study of interventions. The ROBINS-I scale is the appropriate assessment tool.

best_practices: Provide the PMID whenever possible for faster and more accurate study design identification through PubMed metadata
Verify the detected study design manually before proceeding with the full bias assessment using the recommended scale
Use the PDF extraction feature for full-text screening when abstracts alone do not provide enough detail about study methodology
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for study designs outside the supported types (RCT, cohort, case-control, cross-sectional, diagnostic accuracy, non-randomised studies)
Do not skip the validation step when the PubMed API returns empty results; always fall back to text analysis for complete coverage
Do not rely on the scale selection as a substitute for reading and understanding the full risk of bias assessment guidelines for each scale

faq.question: What is a risk of bias assessment scale?
faq.answer: A risk of bias assessment scale is a structured tool used to evaluate the quality and validity of a medical study. Different study designs require different scales.

faq.question: Can this skill perform the full risk of bias assessment?
faq.answer: No. This skill only selects the appropriate assessment scale based on study design. The actual bias assessment must be completed by the researcher using the recommended scale.

faq.question: What happens if the PubMed API is unavailable?
faq.answer: The skill falls back to text analysis of the abstract or study description to identify the study design. This works well for clear study designs but may be less precise.

faq.question: Which assessment scales are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports RoB 2 for RCTs, Newcastle-Ottawa Scale for cohort and case-control studies, ROBINS-I for non-randomised interventions, ROBINS-E for exposure studies, QUADAS-2 for diagnostic studies, and AHRQ or JBI for cross-sectional studies.

faq.question: Does this skill work with non-English studies?
faq.answer: The skill uses PubMed metadata which is primarily in English. Text analysis for study design keywords may not work reliably for non-English abstracts.

faq.question: Is an internet connection required?
faq.answer: Yes, when using the PMID lookup feature. The PubMed metadata fetch requires internet access. Text-only analysis and PDF extraction work offline.
